rulings.Case Title: RACHEL T. LIN VS. JEFFREY CHEN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 11, 2025. Line 3. DEFENDANT BRIAN MI MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT; is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden. The Court notes that defendant only moved for summary judgment, not for summary adjudication. Separate Statement appears to repeat the same 10 facts 10 times. Motion is supported only by declaration of Brain Mi. Paragraph 4 of Mi declaration lacks foundation. This paragraph is listed as the sole source of evidence of Fact 5. No evidence whatsoever is provided by the moving party that the fire was not caused by Mi's "family, invitees, and guests." Furthermore, there is a triable issue of fact regarding the responsible party for the damages caused by fire in the leased unit. =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: GARY ERWIN VS. BRIDGE HOUSING CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Filed Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions, Issue Sanctions, Monetary Sanctions, And Order To Show Cause Re: Contempt
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 11, 2025. Line 4. PLAINTIFF GARY ERWIN Re-Filed Motion For Terminating Sanctions, Issue Sanctions, Monetary Sanctions, And Order To Show Cause Re: Contempt; is DENIED. Based on the evidence presented neither sanctions nor OSC re: contempt are warranted. Moving papers are almost completely devoid of legal authorities in support of plaintiff's position. Reliance on Stephen Slesinger, Inc v. Walt Disney Co. (2007) 155 Cal.App. 4th 736 is misplaced. No evidence presented that Defendants violated CCP 1209(a)(5). To the extent Plaintiff claims that Defendants failed to file a supplemental brief in response to this Court's October 3, 2025 Order, this contention is false. Supplemental brief was filed on October 6, 2025, and considered by the Court as is evidenced by the Court's October 10, 2025 ruling. It appears that reply relies on additional purported violations of procedural requirements arising out of the events that occurred after the motion was filed (proposed orders after demurrer and motion to strike November 5, 2025 hearing). These new arguments are not proper as Defendant has no opportunity to respond. Additionally, failure to comply with CRC 3.1312 does not give rise to any of the sanctions requested by the Plaintiff in this motion. =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
